## FAQs

**How much does it cost?**  
Prices vary, depending on what you choose to make. We don’t charge any studio or firing fees, so the price you see includes everything but sales tax!  
Pottery projects are $14 to $139 per piece, with many of our customers' favorite pieces (like mugs, plates and animals) in the $25 to $35 range. Glass Fusing projects are $17 to $54, and most Wood Signs are $37 to $57.

**Can I bring in pottery I purchased somewhere else?**  
Due to quality issues, we cannot glaze and fire pottery that was not purchased at our studio.

**I have some of my own clay. If I make some pottery will you fire it for me?**  
We're sorry, we are unable to fire clay projects that were not created in our studio.

**How long does it take to make something?**  
Most people spend about 1 to 1½ hours at the studio. Plan on less time if you have young kids or are making a simple project, more time if you're w...[truncated 1762 chars]... products are non-toxic and safe to use with food. In fact, The Mud Room has **only used lead-free glazes** since it opened!

**Can I put my pieces in the dishwasher or microwave?**  
We recommend that you do not put your hand-painted pottery in either the dishwasher or microwave.

**Is the studio a good place to bring my kids?**  
Of course! ALL ages are welcome for pottery painting. Just remember, really young kids may not last very long, and it may be difficult to work on your own project while helping little ones. If you can’t finish your project in the same time you can take it home and finish later!  
Also watch for special events, like Story Time and Camp Mud, that are just for kids! Some activities, like the Potter’s Wheel, do have a minimum age requirement of 7 years.

**I don't live in near Grand Rapids. Where can I find a studio near me?**  
Check out [www.paintyourownpottery.net](http://www.paintyourownpottery.net/) to search for CCSA member studios in your area."

## Wheel Throwing Workshop

In our Intro to Wheel Throwing Workshop, you will learn the basics of centering the clay on the wheel, opening the center and pulling the walls to create shape and depth.

Come, bring friends, and have a relaxing, creative couple of hours getting your hands dirty and playing with clay!

Full schedule of Wheel Workshop and booking options is listed below.

$65 per participant

## Workshops Include

- 1.5 hours of professional instruction and guidance
- Glaze color of your choice (We will glaze your pot once it is bisque-fired.)

- All firing and glazing fees, including bisque firing and glaze firing

- Access to studio tools, etc.
- Guaranteed one successful pot

## Workshop Outline

We will start with a short tour of the space, where to find everything, as well as where things go for clean up. Then we will walk y...[truncated 93 chars]...ur clay and getting the right tools for the job.  
After the introduction, we will demo throwing on the wheel for attendees, and talking through focus areas.

Off to the wheel you go, and you begin centering your clay and moving toward creating your piece.

After throwing for a bit, we clean up and wrap up the workshop with glaze choices. You will pick a glaze for each of your pieces that you decide to keep.

- Sharing Introductions, as well as taking a short studio tour
- Prepping the clay and getting a demo
- Throwing on the wheel
- Cleaning up / finishing up / Choosing glazes for thrown pieces
- Answering any final questions, and we’re done!

## What We Do

At The Clayground, we’re passionate about providing a creative and welcoming space for potters of all levels in West Michigan. Our studio offers a variety of options to explore your artistic side, from monthly memberships with 24/7 access to immersive 6-week classes. Whether you’re a seasoned potter or just starting your journey, we have something to offer.

Our one-time experiences allow you to dip your toes into the world of pottery with a 1.5-2 hour session where you can hand build a unique piece or learn the art of throwing on the wheel. We believe in fostering a supportive community where everyone feels inspired and empowere...[truncated 1256 chars]...offering beginner’s hand building and beginner’s wheel throwing classes to start and will add more classes as we get our bearings.

---

### Monthly Membership

For a monthly fee, members will have 24/7 access to the studio with their own, unique door code. We offer a safe and secure space to create on your own time! Firing will be taken care of by staff and firing fees will be charged separately at a discounted, member rate.

### Private Events

Need a place to celebrate your child’s birthday? Want to have a unique date night? Do you need a fun team-building activity? The Clayground can host special events for ages 6+ and will tailor the experience to fit your needs!

### Specialty Workshops

A goal at The Clayground is to offer unique workshops. These workshops will usually be two sessions, a week apart from one another. We also offer workshops on taking photos for social media, what to expect at markets, and other hot, pottery topics.

#### Welcome!

## Cultivate

## Our Legacy Continues in Arts Advocacy, Education, and Community

Welcome to Cultivate, an independent, artist-run arts organization that served as a physical space for creativity, learning, and community in Grand Rapids for nearly three years. During our time as a local space, we brought together artists, educators, and advocates from across the nation, hosting hundreds of classes, exhibitions, and programs focused on inclusive art education and advocacy.

Now, as we transition to a digital platform, Cultivate continues as both a tribute to our legacy and a call for change. Our online presence offers accessible resources, live classes, and professional tools designed to empower emerging and midcareer artists, art educators, and advocates.

Join us in this next chapter, where we offer strategic resources, insights, and educational opportunities that highlight the value of the arts and advocate for sus...[truncated 1587 chars]...t and inspire leaders, advocates, and those advancing the arts. Whether building an arts space, advocating policy change, or exploring leadership, our tools empower you to make a meaningful impact.

Discover articles, beginner guides, and resources that explore the history, techniques, and cultural significance of art, all designed to deepen your understanding and appreciation of the arts’ role in society and personal growth.

## Classes at the Studio

Welcome to the studio, where creativity meets community! We offer classes for artists of all ages and skill levels, whether you’re just beginning your journey or looking to enhance your existing skills. Our classes are engaging, set in a warm and supportive environment that encourages you to explore your unique gifts and connect with the beauty of creation.

At Little Fine Arts Studio, we pride ourselves on offering smaller class sizes to provide a more intimate and personalized art experience. Our approach ensures all participants receive more one-on-one guidance from the instructor, fostering creativity and building confidence.

Join us and experience the joy of creating in a warm, fun, creative studio setting! Start your artistic journey today and discover the joy that comes from creating with your hands.

### Student Classes

Our student art classes are designed to spark creativity, build skills, explore their artistic talents, express themselves and grow in confidence all while learning new techniques in a supportive space. We offer a variety of engaging lessons from painting, drawing, sculpting, wood burning, 3D pen art, fiber arts.

[Get Started](/classes/)

### Adult Classes

Our adult classes offer a creative escape, perfect for beginners or experienced artists looking to refine their skills. Each session encourages creativity and exploration in a variety of art mediums such as pottery, painting, watercolor, plein air painting, chalk / oil pastels, fiber arts and drawing.

[Get Started](/adult-art-classes/)

### Workshops

Our studio offers unique workshops and events designed to provide a creative outlet for individuals of all skill levels. These special sessions range from seasonal art themes to exploration of new mediums allowing participants to experiment, learn and grow in a fun, relaxed atmosphere with others.

[Get Started](/events/)"

Whether you’re brand new to sewing or have already tackled a few projects, our classes aim to cultivate confidence in your craft and empower you with the knowledge to build your own handmade wardrobe.

---

### HOW IT WORKS

---

Spend your weekend mornings at our beautiful studio space, drinking coffee, meeting local creatives, and learning something new.

At Sew Club, we aim to make sewing approachable, easy, and fun, which is why **everything you need is included in our workshops**!

We offer one-day project workshops, multi-session garment making courses, and a learn to sew introductory class.

Each sewist is set up with a Viking Onyx 25 sewing machine, their own set of heavy duty sewing tools, a spacious workspace, and a menu of high-quality fabrics and notions to complete their project with.

From learning the basics of threading your machine, to sewing on buttonholes, to reading and understanding patterns, our classes will leave you with a solid sewing foundation that will keep you inspired to keep creating.

Discover our upcoming workshops here:

## FAQs

Do you offer refunds if I can’t attend a workshop that I registered for?

We get it….life happens. But we’re also a small business that relies on each ticket sale that comes through. **We have a “no refund” policy but please keep reading to see what we will do for you…..**

Because there is a lot of work that goes in to prepping for your specfic project that you choose, we are happy to transfer your ticket to the *same* future workshop (or you can gift your ticket to someone!). If that is not possible, we can transfer to any other workshop.

Here’s the fine print (sorry but we have to have it!):

Each workshop has different cancellation periods…please see your specific workshop info for details. IN GERNERAL: please provide at least a 72 hour notice for a cancellation so we can open your seat to others. We will gladly transfer your ticket to the same upcoming workshop when you cancel.

Any guest that is a...[truncated 7450 chars]...h space between the leaves, it needs more sunlight (it’s stretching because it is “looking” for light). Here’s a great link to follow for more info on this: [hearthandvine.com](https://hearthandvine.com/succulents-grows-tall/)

*SCORCHING –* Dark, burnt spots on your succulent’s leaves are a sign of too much direct sunlight. These “burns” will not go away, but your succulent will eventually shed these leaves as it grow. To fix this issue, simply place your succulent in non-direct, but bright sunlight.

*WILTED LEAVES –* Wilted, rubbery leaves are a sign of under-watering. Increae the amount of water and frequency until you find your plant’s sweet spot.

*SOGGY/YELLOW LEAVES* or *ROT –* This is typically a sign that you are over-watering your little guy. The best way to save a succulent that has been over-watered is to transfer your succulent to completely dry cactus/succulent soil (do not use regular houseplant soil). After that, cut back how much/often you water your succulent."

Age Minimum: 10 years + to use the wheel
